Conference Highlights

Three days of science · 1–3 July 2026 · Mloganzila Campus

Day 1 · 11:10
Keynote Address: Reorganising Health Systems in Africa — Prioritising Innovation and Research for Evolving Global Health Challenges
Prof. Nathanael Sirili · Ali Hassan Mwinyi Auditorium
Day 2 · 08:00
Plenary: Following the Newborn Journey — Strengthening Newborns Respiratory Care Through the Breath for Babies (BfB) Package in a Hub-and-Spoke Network
Prof. Nahya Salim · Ali Hassan Mwinyi Hall (Chair: Prof. Billy Ngassala · Rapp: Joel Mpoyiki)
Day 3 · 08:30
Keynote Address: Pandemic Preparedness
Dr. Juma Mfinanga · Ali Hassan Mwinyi Auditorium (Chair: Prof. Eligius Lyamuya · Rapp: Joel Mpoyiki)

MUHAS 2026
14th MUHAS Scientific Conference
Innovating for Resilient and Sustainable Universal Health Coverage
1–3 July 2026 · Mloganzila Campus, Dar es Salaam

Welcome

Karibu — welcome to the 14th MUHAS Scientific Conference, hosted by Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences at the Mloganzila Campus, Dar es Salaam.

Over three days you will join keynote and plenary lectures, parallel scientific sessions, satellite symposia and poster presentations spanning research across the health sciences in Tanzania and the region.

Use this app to browse the full programme, explore every oral and poster abstract, read speaker profiles, save sessions to your personal agenda, and ask the support assistant any question about the conference.

Organised by
Directorate of Research, Publications and Innovations (DRPI), MUHAS
Organising Committee Chair: Prof. Raphael Z. Sangeda — Chair, Conference Organising Committee
Guest of Honour: Prof. Adolf Mkenda — Minister for Education, Science and Technology
Conference Theme
"Innovating for Resilient and Sustainable Universal Health Coverage in the Era of Shifting Global Health Financing"
1–3 July 2026 · Mloganzila Campus, Dar es Salaam
← Speakers
14th MUHAS Scientific Conference
Innovating for Resilient and Sustainable Universal Health Coverage
1–3 July 2026 · Mloganzila Campus, Dar es Salaam
Keynote & Plenary Speakers

Tap a speaker to read their full biography

Featured Speakers & Panellists
NS
PROFESSOR NATHANAEL SIRILI
Featured Speaker
▼
Nathanael Sirili is an Associate Professor of Health Systems and Policy at the Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences and a leading implementation scientist, mixed-methods researcher, and health systems expert with over 15 years of experience in research, monitoring and evaluation, policy analysis, and health systems strengthening across Sub-Saharan Africa. He graduated with a Doctor of Medicine (MD) in 2011 and a Master of Science in Health Systems in 2014 from Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences. He obtained his PhD in Public Health (Health Systems) from Umeå University, Sweden, in 2018.

Nathanael has extensive experience working with Ministries of Health and the PMO- RALG in Mainland Tanzania, and with the Ministry of Health, Zanzibar, on health workforce reforms, continuing professional development, emergency care systems, digital health, and primary healthcare strengthening initiatives. He has led and evaluated health programs funded by UNICEF, GIZ, the World Bank, the World Health Organization, AMREF Health Africa, the United States Agency for International Development, the Abbott Fund, the Global Fund, and the Fogarty International Center. With over 100 publications in international peer-reviewed journals, he is widely recognised for his scholarly leadership, mentorship, and contributions to evidence-informed policy and implementation science in low-resource settings.
JM
DR. JUMA MFINANGA
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Juma Mfinanga is an Emergency Medicine Consultant and the head of the Emergency Medicine Department at Muhimbili National Hospital (MNH) in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. He joined MNH in 2010 and was among the pioneers of the Emergency Medicine Department. He was one of the first graduates of the Emergency Medicine Residency at MUHAS, where he now serves as an adjunct faculty member. He is also an adjunct Assistant Professor of Emergency Medicine at Atrium Health in North Carolina, USA.

He is a founding member and currently serves as President of the Emergency Medicine Association of Tanzania (EMAT), the national professional body dedicated to advancing emergency care in Tanzania. In this capacity, he works closely with the Ministry of Health to promote the development and implementation of emergency care systems across all levels of the health system. Dr Mfinanga's academic and research interests include strengthening emergency care systems, emergency medical services, disease outbreaks, disaster medicine, and trauma care. He has authored numerous peer-reviewed publications in both international and national journals. He has contributed significantly to advancing emergency medicine and emergency care systems in Tanzania and the region.
NM
PROF. NAHYA SALIM MASOUD
Featured Speaker
▼
Prof. Nahya Salim Masoud is a Professor of Paediatrics and Director of Research, Publications and Innovation at Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS), Tanzania. She is a clinician-scientist with extensive experience in maternal, newborn, and child health, clinical trials, implementation research, and health systems strengthening. Prof. Salim serves as Clinical Lead, Co-Principal Investigator, and Co-Chair of the NEST360 Alliance, a multicounty initiative improving care for small and sick newborns across Africa. Her work focuses on newborn survival, strengthening research capacity, knowledge translation, and translating evidence into policy and practice.
MM
DR. MARY MWANYIKA
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Mary Mwanyika Sando is Chief Executive Officer of the Africa Academy for Public Health (AAPH) in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. A medical doctor with an MD from the University of Dar es Salaam and a Master of Public Health in Global Health and Population from Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, she brings over 19 years of experience in maternal, child and adolescent health, nutrition, mental health and program implementation with organisations like UNICEF and MDH. At AAPH, Dr. Sando leads research, capacity building, and evidence-to-policy translation to advance public health priorities within and outside Tanzania. Together with the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, Dr Sando co-convenes the Africa Research Implementation Science and Education (ARISE) Network, which links 22+ institutions across 10 sub-Saharan African countries.

Dr Sando also serves as President of the Medical Women Association of Tanzania (MEWATA) and as a member of the International Advisory Boards of the Charité Centre for Global Health and The Lancet Regional Health – Africa.
MA
DR. MARYAM AMOUR
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr Amour is a physician-scientist at Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS), Tanzania, where she also serves as the head of Internationalisation and Convocation. Her professional interests focus on infectious diseases, particularly TB/HIV, adolescent health, and global health security.

Currently, Dr. Amour is a Principal Investigator on a National Institutes of Health (NIH)-funded grant examining new methods for active case finding for tuberculosis (TB) among adolescents and their household contacts. She previously served as a co-investigator in a Phase 2 vaccine clinical trial for the prevention of TB infection among adolescents in Tanzania. Dr Amour has also conducted multiple epidemiological studies on adolescents living with HIV. For the past two years, she has been involved in enhancing country-level analytical capacity and data use in HIV, TB, and malaria programs under the Global Fund.

Dr. Amour is the President Elect of the Medical Women’s Association of Tanzania (MEWATA) and has actively participated in organizing nationwide mass screening campaigns for breast and cervical cancer for over 10 years. Dr Amour has also been involved in strengthening the capacity of civil society organisations (CSOs) working

on entrepreneurship, climate change, women’s rights, and the prevention of gender- based violence in Tanzania. Dr Amour serves as a board member of the Benjamin Mkapa Foundation and the Dartmouth Institute Vaccine Initiative, and as a member of the technical advisory panel of the Pandemic Fund under the World Bank. Dr Amour is also a member of the Association of Commonwealth Universities' health expert advisory group.

Dr Amour holds a degree in Medicine and a Master's in Internal Medicine from MUHAS. Additionally, she holds a Master's degree in Public Health from the Dartmouth Institute for Health Policy and Clinical Practice in the USA. Dr Amour completed a Clinical Trials fellowship at Harvard Medical School and a postdoctoral fellowship in the Department of Global Health and Population at the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health.
MA
PROF. MUZDALIFAT SALIM ABEID
Featured Speaker
▼
Prof. Muzdalifat Salim Abeid is a Senior Consultant Obstetrician and Gynaecologist and Associate Professor at Aga Khan University, Tanzania, bringing over 20 years of clinical and leadership experience in maternal and reproductive health. She currently serves as Medical Director and Head of the Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at Aga Khan Hospital, Dar es Salaam. Prior to this, she served as Head of the Obstetrics and Gynaecology Department at Temeke Regional Referral Hospital, where she led high-volume maternal health services within one of Tanzania's major public referral systems.

Her clinical expertise spans maternal and newborn health, emergency obstetric care, and reproductive health service delivery, with a strong focus on improving outcomes in resource-limited settings.

Beyond clinical practice, Prof. Abeid serves as Head of Women's Healthcare in Africa at Almazina Ltd, where she leads strategic development in women-centred healthcare, innovation, and research. In this role, she is spearheading the advancement of digital health solutions aimed at transforming access to care, strengthening continuity of services, and improving health outcomes for women across the continent, bridging clinical insight with innovation to position women's health as a key driver of scalable, technology-enabled impact in Africa's evolving health systems. She has contributed to major international research and implementation programmes, including the WOMAN Trial, ALERT Project, SaferBirth, and MOYO initiatives, with a focus on translating evidence into stronger maternal health systems. She is actively engaged in medical education, mentorship, and health system strengthening aligned with the Ministry of Health's national priorities, particularly in reducing maternal mortality and improving emergency obstetric care. Prof. Abeid serves on the Editorial Board of BMC Pregnancy and Childbirth and is a member of both AGOTA, MEWATA and ECSACOG. She was honoured with the Aga Khan University Distinction Award in 2025 in recognition of her outstanding contributions to clinical leadership and the advancement of maternal health.
MN
DR. MATILDA NGARINA
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Matilda Ngarina is a Consultant Obstetrician and Gynaecologist at Muhimbili National Hospital (MNH) with over 25 years of experience in clinical practice and research. She holds a PhD in Reproductive Health from Karolinska Institutet, Sweden, and a Master of Medicine in Obstetrics and Gynaecology from Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS). A distinguished leader in her field, Dr. Ngarina is the immediate past President of the Association of Gynaecologists and Obstetricians of Tanzania (AGOTA) and a Fellow of the East, Central and Southern African College of Obstetrics and Gynaecology (ECSACOG).

Her research spans reproductive health and intrapartum care, with particular focus on infertility, reproductive choices, preterm births, foetal and maternal surveillance, and Prevention of Mother-to-Child Transmission (PMTCT) services. She has authored more than 25 publications in peer-reviewed journals and has collaborated with local and international partners across these areas.

Dr. Ngarina also contributes to academic medicine as a Senior Honorary Lecturer in the Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at MUHAS, where she has mentored numerous postgraduate students at both Masters and PhD levels. Dr Ngarina also served as an executive committee member of the Medical Women Association of Tanzania (MEWATA).
LM
DR. LILIAN REMIGIUS MNABWIRU
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Lilian Remigius Mnabwiru is a Senior Obstetrician and Gynaecologist at Muhimbili National Hospital (MNH), with 16 years of experience in obstetrics, gynaecology, and reproductive medicine. She holds an MD from Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS) and a Master of Medicine (MMed) from Kilimanjaro Christian Medical University College (KCMC University), with a Certificate in Reproductive Endocrinology and Infertility. Dr. Mnabwiru has pursued advanced training across multiple international institutions, reflecting her commitment to excellence and continuous professional development.

Her qualifications include an Advanced Diploma in Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) from Lund University, Sweden; an Advanced Diploma in Assisted Reproductive Technology from BACC Healthcare, Bangalore, India; a Leadership and Management Course from the University of Stellenbosch, South Africa; and specialised clinical training through the Dr. Lee Jong-wook Fellowship Programme at Yonsei University, South Korea.

Her clinical and research interests include new reproductive technologies, women's health and imaging, reproductive endocrinology, endoscopic surgery, and sexual and reproductive health research. Dr Mnabwiru has also served in various leadership roles with the Medical Association of Tanzania (MAT), the Medical Women Association of Tanzania (MEWATA), and the Association of Gynaecologists and Obstetricians of Tanzania (AGOTA).

In addition to her clinical role, Dr. Mnabwiru serves as an Honorary Lecturer at MUHAS, where she supervises both undergraduate and postgraduate students. She is a proud mother of two daughters.
JL
DR. JOHNSON LYMO
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr Johnson Lymo is a global health professional with over 20 years of experience in the programmatic control of tuberculosis and TB/HIV at the national level. Currently, he supports the Ministry of Health in scaling up combination HIV prevention strategies, including differentiated service delivery for HIV self-testing, sustainable VMMC, PrEP utilisation, and integrated HIV/TB/hepatitis programming.

Dr Lyimo has extensive expertise in national policy and program planning, donor coordination, and external program evaluations. My technical focus is on advancing evidence-based practices for HIV, TB, and hepatitis in sub-Saharan Africa.
ET
ELIZABETH A. TALBOT
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Talbot is an internist with subspecialty training in infectious diseases and tropical and travel medicine, who has spent her career focused on tuberculosis. She has extensive experience in international and domestic infectious disease control through outbreak investigation, clinical projects, research, and consultation. Dr. Talbot trained at Duke University and with the CDC's Epidemic Intelligence Service then directed CDC TB-HIV research in Botswana. She was then assigned to the World Health Organization in Geneva before coming to Dartmouth where she is a Section Chief and Professor of Medicine in the Section of Infectious Diseases and International Health. Since 2003, Dr Talbot has been New Hampshire’s Deputy State Epidemiologist; she consulted with the Foundation for Innovative New Diagnostics in Geneva from 2007- 2011, and since January 2020, she has worked with Gates MRI toward the identification of TB treatment-monitoring biomarkers and the development of improved TB treatments.
DM
PROF. DOREEN MLOKA
Featured Speaker
▼
Prof. Doreen Mloka is an Associate Professor in the Department of Pharmaceutical Microbiology, School of Pharmacy, MUHAS. Her research interests include antimicrobial resistance, infectious diseases, and health profession education. She is the current chair of the Clinical Trials Committee at the Tanzania Drug and Medical Authority (TMDA).

At MUHAS, she has held the posts of Director of Continuing Education and Professional Development, a MUHAS curriculum chair, a head of department, and the Scientific Chair of the MUHAS annual scientific conference for 10 years.

SIANA WATOKY NKY BSC., MSC., PHD Dr Nkya is a Senior lecturer in the Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology at Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS). For the past 15 years, she has gathered research experience in biomedical science, particularly in molecular biology and human genetics. Dr Nkya’s research focuses on applying human molecular and genomic skills to disease prevention, diagnosis, understanding disease mechanisms, discovery and development of interventions (drugs and gene-based therapies), and monitoring treatment outcomes (pharmacogenomics).

She has primarily applied her skills to malaria and sickle cell disease (SCD). During her early training, she conducted malaria research, focusing on molecular resistance markers of drug resistance in P. falciparum. For her PhD training, she conducted research in SCD genomics. In 2014, she led the establishment of a genetic database of more than 1,700 individuals with SCD, one of the first in Africa.

Throughout her career, Dr Nkya has worked closely with the Molecular Haematology Department at King's College London, the Medical Genetics Group at the Wellcome Trust Sanger Institute, the Division of Haematology/Oncology at Boston Children's Hospital, and the Department of Applied Sciences at the University of Northumbria. Dr Nkya was part of the team that established the newborn screening program for SCD in Tanzania, gaining experience in setting up a screening and diagnostic laboratory. Dr Nkya has received various research support, including the Fogarty Global Health Fellowship (2016) and the Emerging Global Leader Award (K43, 2019- 2024) from the NIH, American Society of Haematology (ASH) Global Research Award (2018) to conduct a study on pharmacogenomics, Novartis Global Health investigators-initiated trial (2022-2024) to scale up the pharmacogenomics study to Ghana and Nigeria, Early Career Women Scientist award from the Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World (OWSD) to develop a minION DNA diagnostic technology for hemoglobinopathies in collaboration with the University of Oxford, UK.

In 2017, Dr Nkya co-founded the Tanzania Human Genetics Organisation (THGO), which is part of the Africa Society of Human Genetics (AfSHG), and currently serves as the first President. Dr Nkya has also worked with the sickle cell disease program in Tanzania for the past 15 years in different capacities and is currently the Co-PI and research technical lead of the Sickle in Africa clinical coordinating centre (SickleInAfrica-CCC), which coordinates research across the Sickle in Africa consortium with sites in seven countries in Africa.
MN
DR. MARIAM NOORANI
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Mariam Noorani is the head of research and the Chair of Department of Paediatrics at the Aga Khan University (AKU) and Aga Khan Health Service, Tanzania (AKHST). She is a paediatrician and International Board-Certified Lactation Consultant (IBCLC) and holds a Master’s degree in Medical Education from the University of Edinburgh. She is also a Senior Fellow of the Higher Education Academy (SFHEA). She is a research fellow with the Aga Khan

University Institute of Global Health and Development (IGHD) Women in Science Program.

She is the founding chair of the Department of Paediatrics at AKU in Tanzania, where she established the residency training program. Under her leadership, the Department has expanded into a comprehensive unit providing the highest standards of care to sick children, including a well-established neonatal unit caring for sick and premature newborns.

In an effort to improve care for children in Tanzania, she led the establishment of a Master’s program at Aga Khan University to train paediatricians, which has now graduated its first cohort of trainees. She also led the introduction of the newborn screening program for sickle cell disease with a pathway into comprehensive care for those who screened positive.

She has developed partnerships with schools and communities to promote child health and nutrition and works with adolescents on healthy behaviours and lifestyles. She has also championed the cause for children with rare diseases in Tanzania. She is a board member of the Ali Kimara Rare Disease Foundation (AKRDF) and the Tanzania Human Genetics Organisation (THGO).

Dr Mariam is actively involved in research and has published several papers and supervised many young researchers. She was awarded a grant to strengthen research capacity through which many doctors and nurses were trained in advancing their research skills.

Her vision is to work with partners to establish a centre of excellence for child health that will engage in clinical care, research and training to improve the health and well-being of children in Tanzania.
CC
DR. CLARA CHAMBA
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr Clara Chamba is a haematologist, senior lecturer, and Head of the Department of Haematology and Blood Transfusion at Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences, with over 8 years of experience in clinical care, research, and postgraduate training. Her work sits at the intersection of clinical care and genomic innovation, with a focus on improving the timely and accurate diagnosis of cancer in resource-limited settings. She has led and contributed to multi-country research collaborations across Tanzania, Uganda and the UK, demonstrating the potential of blood-based genomic approaches, including liquid biopsy, to reduce diagnostic delays and support earlier treatment decisions for patients with lymphoma. Her work has been instrumental in building local expertise and driving the integration of genomic technologies into routine clinical care across the region.
RS
PROF. RAPHAEL ZOZIMUS SANGEDA
Featured Speaker
▼
Professor Raphael Zozimus Sangeda is a Professor in the Department of Pharmaceutical Microbiology, the Acting Principal of the MUHAS-Kigoma Campus (MKC), and the Director of Continuing Education and Professional Development (DCEPD) at Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS). His work focuses on pharmaceutical informatics and healthcare data science, employing advanced computational tools to model public health issues.

Professor Sangeda has extensive expertise in applying geospatial analysis and machine learning models to track HIV care retention, oversee antimicrobial stewardship, and map viral phylogeography across transit corridors. He actively collaborates with national regulatory bodies and international academic institutions to enhance bioinformatics capacity, connecting molecular genomics with actionable national health policy. He has served as the Chair of MUHAS Scientific Conference Organising Committee from 2024 to the present.
WO
MR. WILLIAM OTUCK
Featured Speaker
▼
William Otuck is a public health practitioner with over a decade of experience working with adolescents and young people in Tanzania and beyond. With a background in clinical medicine and business administration, he has supported initiatives in youth wellbeing, SRHR, safeguarding, and systems strengthening at community, national, and international levels.

Currently at YLabs, Otuck works on research and innovation projects that advance adolescent protection and equity. He is passionate about survivor-centred responses, prevention, and creating systems that protect children and young people from sexual violence.
AK
DR. ALEXANDER KAILEMBO
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Alexander Kailembo is a public health specialist with expertise in surveillance, implementation science, and epidemiologic research. He serves as Team Lead for Epidemiology and Surveillance at CDC Tanzania, where he has led national surveys, including the Tanzania HIV Impact Survey and the Violence Against Children and Youth Survey, as well as surveillance systems and special studies.

His work focuses on generating high-quality evidence to inform policy, strengthen public health programs, and improve health outcomes and well-being in Tanzania.
SS
DR. SANG'UDI SANG'UDI
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Sang’udi Sang’udi is a visionary Tanzanian medical doctor, health data scientist, and social entrepreneur with over six years of experience transforming healthcare through artificial intelligence (AI). He graduated from the Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS), one of East Africa’s premier health sciences institutions, where he earned his medical degree and laid the foundation for his career at the intersection of medicine and technology.

As a co-founder of SarataniAI, a Google Developer-funded initiative, he led the team in building an AI-powered cervical cancer screening solution designed for underserved communities. His team has amassed a locally collected dataset of over 1.1 million cervical images, one of the most significant cervical cancer imaging datasets on the African continent, enabling the development of robust, context- specific diagnostic AI tools.

Dr. Sang’udi is a 2025 Mandela Washington Fellow, the flagship program of the Young African Leaders Initiative (YALI), where he was hosted at the University of Nevada, Reno, on the Business and Entrepreneurship track. This prestigious program sharpened his entrepreneurial acumen and shaped his journey as a health technology entrepreneur in the United States, equipping him with global business leadership skills to scale impactful innovations across Africa. He is a Commonwealth Startup Fellow and an alumnus of the Simon Imbizo Computational Neuroscience School in South Africa, Dr. Sang’udi brings a rare combination of clinical expertise, computational fluency, and entrepreneurial vision. He also leads a C-THAN-funded project developing AI-powered screening tools for women living with HIV/AIDS.

He has been selected for the Mathematical Bridging Program for Machine Learning and Computational Neuroscience at University College London (UCL), which he will attend this summer, further deepening his mastery of the mathematical foundations underpinning modern AI systems.

Grounded in ethical AI and human-centred design, Dr Sang’udi Sang’udi is shaping a healthier, more equitable future for Africa — one innovation at a time.
DM
DR. DEGRATIUS MZURIKWAO
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr Deogratias Mzurikwao received his PhD from the University of Kent in 2020, specialising in the applications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare. He holds a Master’s degree in AI from Tianjin University in China. Since 2012, he has been actively engaged in research and practice in machine learning, deep learning, generative AI, and signal processing for both images and voice—core pillars of modern AI systems.

During his studies in China, he worked as a data scientist at GTA, a leading big data analytics company based in Shenzhen, which recruited him while he was still completing his master’s degree. In 2014, he and his teammate secured third place in an international innovation competition for automation and UAV technologies. In 2019, he was one of only 15 AI researchers globally to receive the IEEE Outstanding Young Investigator Research Visit Award, which enabled a research residency at Sapienza University in Italy.

Dr. Deogratias’s research interests include deep learning, generative AI, machine learning, data analytics, explainable AI, super-resolution, and immersive technologies such as virtual and augmented reality. He has published widely in peer- reviewed journals including Nature and IEEE, with over 2,653 citations, an h-index of 11, and an i10-index of 13. His work has been featured in major international media outlets, including Forbes and the BBC.

He has provided AI consulting services for organisations such as UNDP, UNICEF, UNU, and the Ministry of Health in Tanzania. For the past three years, he has served as the AI Sector Lead at Villgro Africa, supporting the development and scale-up of AI-driven innovations, including generative AI solutions, across the African continent.

Dr. Deogratias currently leads the Emerging Technologies for Health (ETH) Lab at Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS), where he drives research and innovation in AI applications for health in low-resource settings.
ER
DR. ERIC RASMUSSEN
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Eric Rasmussen is the Principal Scientist and Research Director for the Kwajalein Atoll Sustainability Laboratory in the Republic of the Marshall Islands, supported by the US Office of Naval Research. He is an Internal Medicine physician with undergraduate and medical degrees from Stanford University and a European Master’s degree in Disaster Medicine from the UN World Health Organisation’s affiliate, CEMEC, in Italy. He serves as an Affiliate Associate Professor of Medicine at the University of Washington (UW) in Seattle. At the 14th MUHAS Scientific Conference, his address focuses on "Applied Complexity Engineering in Public Health: The CASA Model in Kigoma.

He served in the US Navy for 25 years aboard nuclear submarines, amphibious ships, heavy cruisers, and aircraft carriers, and his wartime deployments include Bosnia three times, Afghanistan twice, and Iraq in 2003. While in uniform, Dr. Rasmussen was appointed a Principal Investigator by DARPA (the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency), leading to his selection as DARPA’s Principal Investigator of the Year in 2003. After retiring from the Navy, he was appointed the Founding CEO of the TED Prize awarded to Dr. Larry Brilliant, then Executive Director of Google.org, and remains Chair of that Board.

In addition to his current work in Ukraine and the Yucatan rainforest of Mexico, Dr. Rasmussen oversees two Climate Adaptation laboratories. One, the Kwajalein Atoll Sustainability Laboratory, is designed for applied research and field experimentation in the Marshall Islands, 2,500 miles west of Hawaii. The other, in partnership with faculty from the Santa Fe Institute, Argonne National Laboratory, and Future Complexity in Valparaiso, Chile, focuses on the use of applied complexity science to mitigate polycrisis vulnerability at the Cape Horn Sub-Antarctic Research Centre in Puerto Williams, Chile – the southernmost permanent human habitation on earth.
HA
MR. HASSAN ALAM
Featured Speaker
▼
Mr. Hassan Alam is an AI entrepreneur and executive with over 25 years of experience in machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and large language models (LLMs).

He is the founder of AI Asset Management and previously served as Chief Innovation Officer at Nitro and Founder/CEO of BCL Technologies. He has secured multiple U.S. government- funded AI research contracts and awards, leading innovations in document intelligence and language technologies. Hassan holds dual bachelor's degrees from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and an MBA from the

Stanford Graduate School of Business. He has pursued advanced studies in AI at the University of Cambridge, MIT, and Stanford.
NB
DR. NICOLE VAN DEN BERG
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Nicoline van den Berg is a physician with a PhD in Clinical Cardiology from the Amsterdam University Medical Centre and with experience in clinical medicine and public health, including work in Malawi and Ghana.

Later this year, she will join the Department of Cardiology at Amsterdam UMC as Scientific Coordinator of a flagship European project focused on the implementation of AI-assisted cardiovascular care and health equity. She also explores with MUHAS the implementation of AI-assisted echocardiography in Tanzania.
HM
DR. HERI A. MARWA
Featured Speaker
▼
Country Director, PharmAccess – Tanzania Dr. Heri Marwa is a seasoned healthcare professional with over 20 years of frontline experience advancing health systems across Africa. A medical doctor by training, Dr Marwa has dedicated his career to pioneering health financing innovations, digital health and scaling inclusive care delivery models that prioritise access and quality for underserved populations. He currently serves as the Country Director for PharmAccess in Tanzania, an international non-profit organisation committed to improving healthcare in sub-Saharan Africa through health system strengthening and digital innovation.

Under his leadership, PharmAccess played a pivotal role in the design, pilot, and scale-up of Tanzania’s Improved Community Health Fund (iCHF) between 2015 and 2018 and in the development of the 2023 Zanzibar Health Insurance Act and the establishment of the Zanzibar Health Services Fund (ZHSF), helping to lay the foundation for more sustainable and equitable healthcare financing. Dr Marwa is also a member of the Africa CDC Experts Working Group (EWG) – Health Innovation and Finance.

Prior to his work with PharmAccess, he served as Operations Manager at AAR Tanzania, a leading private health insurance provider. Dr. Marwa brings a unique blend of medical, policy, and operational expertise, and continues to be a strong advocate for health equity through systemic innovation.
EK
PROFESSOR EMERITUS JAPHET KILLEWO
Featured Speaker
▼
Japhet Killewo (JK) is a professor emeritus at the Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS) in Tanzania, where he has been teaching epidemiology. He does research in communicable and non-communicable diseases and has continued to do so to date. He also worked for 4 years with the International Centre for Diarrhoeal Disease Research (ICDDRB) in Bangladesh, monitoring and evaluating reproductive health interventions in a health and demographic surveillance system. JK initiated an urban-based health and demographic

surveillance platform in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, in 2010, where more than 100,000 residents of all ages are being followed for in- and out-migration, vital events, socio- economic status, NCDs, food insecurity and HIV Drug Resistance. This unique cohort provides policymakers and scientists with accurate, reliable data.

JK was party to launching the One Health agenda in Tanzania. JK is currently the Country Manager of Africa One Health University Network (AFROHUN) in Tanzania. In this position JK carries out One Health activities in collaboration with human and animal health institutions and government agencies in Tanzania and as coordinated by the Prime Minister’s Office.

JK has critically reviewed a considerable number of research proposals and manuscripts for various peer-reviewed international journals and has published widely in local and international journals, having written chapters in books and edited a section in the International Encyclopaedia of Public Health. He is also the Editor-in-Chief of the derivative volume on Epidemiology and Demography in Public Health and edited and contributed a chapter to the book “Primary Health Care in Tanzania through a Health Systems Lens: A History of the Struggle for Universal Health Coverage”, September 2023. JK has more than 130 published articles in journals to his credit as of June 2024. Email: jkillewo@yahoo.co.uk Link to my publications:
AP
PROFESSOR ANDREA B. PEMBE
Featured Speaker
▼
Professor Andrea B. Pembe, MD, MMed (UDSM), PhD (Upsalla), FCOG (ECSA) is a medical doctor specialised in global maternal and reproductive health. He is a Consultant and a Professor of Obstetrics and Gynaecology, a Former Vice Chancellor (2018-2023) of Muhimbili University of Health and Allied Sciences, Dar es Salaam, and a member of several Professional and Advisory Boards in Tanzania and beyond. Andrea has more than 30 years of experience in reproductive health service provision and training. He has successfully implemented sponsored and collaborative projects across diverse environments, demonstrating the ability to maximise accountability and effectiveness in designing and implementing policies and innovative practices, thereby mitigating operational risks.

He has also worked extensively with other development partners—Swedish International Development Agency (Sida), Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (BMGF), National Institutes of Health (NIH), Clinton Health Access Initiative, Global Fund through the Ministry of Health, and many others. In all implementations, the Ministries of Health, Education and the Prime Minister’s Office - Regional Administration and Local Government (PMO-RALG). To this end, research and implementation work have been key to his strong ties at the National, regional, district and health facility levels, as well as in communities.

Currently, some of the research and interventions Andrea is involved in include maternal and newborn knowledge synthesis to guide policy, practice and health system strengthening (MUHAS Knowledge – Translation-Hub), Scaling up of Multiple Micronutrient Supplementation in pregnancy (MMS-Scaling) in Pregnancy, Optimising safe caesarean section (C-SAFE), MMS postpartum for neurodevelopment (MMS-POST)

and Validation of screening tools for preeclampsia in Africa (PROTECT-Africa).
DP
MS. DATIVA PEREUS
Featured Speaker
▼
Ms Dative Pereus is a PhD candidate in bioinformatics at the University of Nairobi, Kenya, and an assistant research fellow at MUHAS. Her research focuses on genomics and bioinformatics, with a strong focus on infectious disease genomic surveillance. She has developed strong expertise in genomic surveillance and bioinformatics workflows, spanning from raw sequencing read processing to the interpretation of complex population-level genetic signals. She is an accomplished scientist with over 30 peer-reviewed publications. Her work has been recognised through several competitive awards and fellowships.

Beyond research, she is deeply committed to capacity building in Africa-led data science, and she has mentored over 10 MSc students and 15 interns in bioinformatics. She delivers hands-on training in next-generation sequencing (NGS) and supports the development and implementation of open-source bioinformatics pipelines.
RM
DR. REDEMPTA J. MBATIA
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Redempta J. Mbatia, MD, MSc Epidemiology, is a Tanzanian physician, epidemiologist, public health leader, and research scientist with more than two decades of experience in public health programming, research, and evidence-informed policy engagement.

She is the Founder and Executive Director of Tanzania Health Promotion Support (THPS), a national NGO supporting government efforts to improve health and social welfare services. Through her leadership, THPS has become a trusted partner in advancing public health interventions and strengthening health systems across Tanzania.

Her work focuses on translating evidence into policy and practice and research to improve outcomes for vulnerable populations, particularly women, children, adolescents, and youth. She has led and contributed to major public health programmes and research, including the Tanzania Second National Survey on Violence Against Children and Youth (VACS 2024).

Dr. Mbatia has published widely on HIV, malaria, maternal health, and quality improvement. Her research and publications continue to inform national and international policy and program implementation.

Dr Mbatia also serves as an adjunct faculty member at MUHAS and contributes to boards that support health systems strengthening and girl-child empowerment.
MA
MS. MIRANDA ARMSTRONG
Featured Speaker
▼
Miranda Armstrong is Chief of Child Protection at UNICEF Tanzania and co-Chair of the Development Partners Group for Gender Equality. She has more than twenty years of experience advancing child rights, child protection systems, social welfare reform, and evidence-based responses to violence against children and gender-based violence.

She has led child protection programmes across Tanzania, Sri Lanka, Côte d’Ivoire, Burundi and West and Central Africa, working closely with governments, civil society, donors, communities and young people to strengthen prevention, response, policy and legislative frameworks. In Tanzania, she has supported national efforts to end violence against women and children, including the launch of national action plans for Mainland Tanzania and Zanzibar. She has contributed to research, data systems and advocacy on violence prevention, alternative care, child marriage, corporal punishment and social welfare strengthening. Miranda brings to the panel a systems perspective on VAWC prevention and response, grounded in practical experience linking policy, services, financing, data and community engagement to protect women, children and adolescents from violence.
LM
PROF. LINDA MHANDO
Featured Speaker
▼
Prof. Linda Mhando is a lawyer and social scientist, and a Senior Research Fellow at the Economic and Social Research Foundation (ESRF) in Tanzania. She specialises in violence against children, gender-based violence, child protection systems, governance, and implementation research.

Prof. Mhando has led multidisciplinary research and advised governments, development partners, and international organisations on translating evidence into policy and practice. Her work spans Tanzania and the broader African region, with a focus on strengthening child protection systems, advancing violence prevention, and promoting evidence- informed policies that improve the lives of women and children.
LT
MS. LUCY TESHA
Featured Speaker
▼
Ms. Lucy Tesha serves as a Program Specialist for Ending Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) at the UN Women Tanzania Country Office, where she leads operations at the Zanzibar sub-office. She brings over 20 years of experience in gender equality and women’s empowerment, having launched local, national, and international initiatives to combat gender- based violence (GBV). Her career features strategic collaborations with governments, local authorities, and development partners to build safer environments and responsive protection systems.

Notably, Ms Tesha has pioneered the establishment of robust local GBV prevention programs; developed vital safety models, including the "Safe Cities Program"; and championed gender-responsive policing and GBV-related multisectoral support systems. She holds a Master’s degree in Development Studies (majoring in Gender

and Development, and State and Civil Society) alongside a Postgraduate degree in Gender and Economic Policy Analysis.
GM
DR. GERRY MSHANA
Featured Speaker
▼
Gerry Mshana is a Principal Research Scientist at the National Institute for Medical Research (NIMR) in Mwanza, Tanzania and a senior researcher at the Mwanza Intervention Trials Unit. He is a medical anthropologist with more than 17 years of research experience on adolescent sexual and reproductive health, the socio-cultural aspects of HIV/AIDS, male circumcision, stroke, parkinson’s disease and epilepsy.

His current research focuses on exploring the structural drivers of HIV, especially alcohol use among young people and gender based violence. He co-chairs the Alcohol Working Group of STRIVE, a health research programme consortium funded by DFID to address structural drivers of the HIV epidemic with partners in Tanzania, South Africa and India. Dr. Mshana is a co-investigator of two cluster randomised controlled trials to assess the impact on women’s experience of intimate partner violence of a combined microfinance and gender training intervention for women; and participatory gender training programme for women and their partners not receiving microfinance. He is also involved in various research capacity-building initiatives in the East African region, such as the Wellcome Trust-funded THRIVE consortium, which involves 6 universities and research institutions in the region and 2 UK institutions (Cambridge University and the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine).
AK
ADV. ANNA KULAYA
Featured Speaker
▼
Anna Meela Kulaya has spent almost twenty years serving her community through her work in the legal sector. Ms. Kulaya graduated with a Bachelor of Laws from the University of Dar es Salaam in 2000 before continuing her education and receiving a diploma in Human Rights and Humanitarian Law from the Raoul Wallenberg Institute in Lund, Sweden, an international diploma in Leadership Development from St. Francis Xavier at the Coady Institute, Canada and a Master in Migration and Refugee Laws from the University of Dar es Salaam. Ms. Kulaya began working for Women in Law and Development in Africa (WiLDAF) in 2001 as a legal.

Now, she is the Deputy National Coordinator of WiLDAF Tanzania, a supervisory role in which she manages a team of 10 people across 10 sub-grant partnerships. Ms Kulaya presently serves as a board member of a network for legal aid providers (TANLAP). Ms Kulaya coordinated the 16 Days of Activism Against GBV in Tanzania, which has since become a national movement.
MM
DR. MOKE MAGOMA
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Moke Magoma oversees EngenderHealth’s diverse portfolio in Tanzania, which includes family planning and sexual and reproductive health, comprehensive abortion care, and HIV and AIDS prevention and care programs. He has more than 20 years of experience in leadership, management, and service delivery of reproductive, maternal, newborn, child, and adolescent health programs, including family planning, in Tanzania. He has

worked for several international organisations in the country, including PEPFAR, OPTIONS UK and GIZ. He was recruited as the SuFP programme country Director in 2019 and promoted early this year to become EngenderHealth Country Representative. He is a reviewer for several international peer-reviewed journals in his field and currently serves as an assistant editor to the Journal of Pregnancy and Childbirth.

Moke earned a Doctor of Medicine degree from the University of Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, a Master of Medicine degree in obstetrics and gynaecology from Makerere University, Kampala, Uganda and a Doctor of Philosophy in epidemiology and population health from the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, University of London, UK. He is married with three children.
FB
DR. FIDELIS BUGOYE
Featured Speaker
▼
Dr. Fidelis Charles Bugoye is a Tanzanian molecular biologist and forensic scientist with expertise in human genetics, DNA technology, and forensic investigations. He is affiliated with the Government Chemist Laboratory Authority (GCLA), Directorate of Forensic Science and DNA Services, where he contributes to the application of DNA technologies in human identification and forensic analysis. (Science Publishing Group)

Dr. Bugoye has contributed to scientific research focusing on forensic DNA identification, including the use of genetic technologies in disaster victim identification and other human identification challenges. His work reflects the growing role of molecular biology and genomics in strengthening scientific evidence systems in Tanzania. (sciencepg.com)

He has also contributed to biomedical research, including studies related to prostate cancer and genetic factors influencing health outcomes. Through his research and professional work, Dr. Bugoye continues to support advancements in forensic science, genetics, and health research in Tanzania and beyond. (pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov).

🔒 Full-version feature
CPD Tracking & Auto Certificates
Per-session QR check-in, automatic CPD point tallying (MCT-aligned) and instant personalised certificates are available in the complete platform.
About CPD points

CPD points for this conference are awarded per Medical Council of Tanganyika (MCT) guidelines and confirmed by the organising committee. Certificates are issued by the secretariat after the event.
